spec_match.rationale: Covers part of the spec (1 of 5 named API surfaces) — distinct angle worth picking up.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered but only a few failure modes are surfaced.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Has 6 raw hex colour value(s) inline — should use --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens instead.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for support-path-attribute-on-svg-textpath-element
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: SVG textPath path attribute. New: path attribute on textPath element accepts a path data string (same syntax as <path d="...">), letting textPath embed its path inline rather than reference an existing <path> via href. Coexists with href (path attribute takes precedence). Also supported: side (left/right), startOffset, spacing (auto/exact), method (align/stretch). Edge cases: invalid path data → t...

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the design-system compliance
openQuestions.detail: Either replace raw hex with --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens, or pair every button background override with a color override (the global button rule sets both — overriding one alone risks light-on-light).

summary: Workable concept with a couple of gaps. 3 open question(s) queued.
